title: Course Overview Derivatives FINS3635 T12025
body:
Course Overview  Basic information

• Lecturer in Charge • Yunshen Yang or (Alex) • Contact information: yunshen.yang@unsw.edu.au

• My Background • B.S. in Statistics and M.S. in Operations Management • Ph.D. in Actuarial Science from UNSW Sydney • Specialization: Derivative pricing and risk management, behavioural finance, model uncertainty…

• TA: • Yiru (Penny) WANG (Ph.D candicate) • Contact information: yiru.wang2@student.unsw.edu.au 2  Basic information

• There are no tutorials for this course

• Weekly Office Hours: • I will hold office hours every Thursday from 3:30 pm to 4:30 pm in the zoom room here. • Closer to the mid-term and final assessments the TA will also have office hours.

• What I expect from you: • Before the class: Read book chapter(s) before the lecture. • During the class: Ask me questions if you do not understand. • After the class: • Do the recommended exercises for each lecture, • Read your notes or the recordings

• To be able to use your calculator and Excel.  About the course • Objective • Introduction to pricing principle and hedging techniques in derivative markets

• Focus on • Standard derivative instruments: Forward, Futures, Swap, and Options

• Topics covered • Pricing of derivatives • Hedging with derivatives • Trading strategies using derivatives

• Why this course is important? Derivatives are everywhere! • Portfolio allocation: Used for hedging market crashes. • Banking: Structured products sold by banks involves derivatives. • Trading: Most trading desks at banks trade derivatives heavily. • Investment banking: Debt and equity are often issued with derivatives attached. • Corporate finance: Companies hedge their cash flows (revenues & costs) using derivatives. • Etc ….

Textbook

• Hull, Options, Futures And Other Derivatives, 8th edition • A pdf version is available in Moodle. • If you use an old/new edition, make sure you read the right chapters and do the correct exercises. • There is solutions provided for the exercises of each chapter. • I will follow the book closely. • Recommended exercises are from the book. • Although I provide you with a pdf copy, you are strongly recommended to get it.

Assessments

• Group Assignment 1 (10%)

• Midterm exam (35%)

• Group assignment 2 (15%)

• Final exam (40%)

• Group Assignment 1 (10%) • Excel • Topic: • Margin Calls (from Week 1) • Minimum Variance Hedge (from Week 2)

• 1-4 students • Release in Week 3 (Friday 11:55pm) • Due in Week 5 (Friday 11:55pm) • More information will be given in class in week 2

• Week 7: Midterm (35%) • You have to take the exam on campus • Open book • 2 hours • Week 7 (1-4 pm, Saturday) • Location: F10 June Griffith M17 (K-F10-M17)

• Covers materials in week 1-5 • Similar to textbook exercise questions • More information will be given in Week 5 or 6.

• Group assignment 2 (15%) • Excel • Topic: • Binomial trees (from Week 5 – Lecture 5) • Black Scholes (from Week 8 – lecture 7)

• 1-4 students, not necessary the same group as assignment 1 • Release in Week 8 (Friday 11:55pm) • Due in Week 10 (Friday 11:55pm) • More information will be given after midterm

• Final (40%) • Time: School exam time • Open book but no electronic devices allowed • 2 hours (like midterm) • Similar to textbook exercise questions • More information will be given in Week 9-10.

Moodle

• Ed Forums: • All announcements and communications will be on the forum. • You can also use it to find teammates for group assignments. • If you have an answer to one of the other student question, share it and help your colleagues! • Make friends, maybe in the future, they will help you get the job you want: Good things happen to good people. • When it comes to final grade, and if you are on the margin, your participation in the forums may push you to the next grade level. Something to keep in mind. • I will oversee them and will try to provide answers in a timely manner (within 48 hours). • I have invited all enrolled students. If you are not invited due to some reasons, please use this link https://edstem.org/au/join/afJCWK to sign up.  Moodle

• My personal email is shared with you for urgent/personal matters • Try my best to reply in 48 hours. • If it is not urgent nor personal, use the Ed forum or ask me your questions at the end of the class or during my office hours.
